name: Blur
post_apply_message:
Blur (2010, Bizarre Creations, non-Steam cracked copy) — Apply auto-adds the Steam shortcut for Blur.exe on GE-Proton 10.34, with a pinned appid so its prefix survives a reimage. Stage GE first with 'gfm stage-runner'.

That's deliberately ALL it does. Blur shipped with Games for Windows Live, which is dead — but your copy has no GFWL bits left at all (no xlive.dll anywhere; the crack is baked into Blur.exe), so there's nothing to bypass. It's a 2010 console-era racer: native widescreen and native Xbox 360 pad support, so Steam Input just works. No mod, no fix, no launch options needed.

LANGUAGE (if you ever want it): the root english.txt is the language selector — rename it to french.txt / italian.txt / german.txt / spanish.txt to switch.

IF IT WON'T LAUNCH: shout — but there's no known Proton blocker here, and nothing exotic in the folder (just Blur.exe, binkw32.dll for Bink video, and data).
notes:
Built 2026-07-13 straight off Tony's Windows install at F:\games\Blur (130 files / 7.3GB), staged to NAS _games/Blur — no SD scan needed.

WHY THERE'S NO GFWL WORK HERE: Blur is a Games for Windows Live title and I originally listed it under 'Tier 1 — dead launcher, needs a GFWL bypass'. That was WRONG for Tony's copy and he called it out: the crack already handles it. Verified against the real install — a recursive search for xlive/GFWL/GameuxInstall/crack/1911 components found NOTHING, and the only exes are Blur.exe (27.6MB) and Uninstall/unins000.exe. So the recipe is a plain shortcut; don't re-add a GFWL step on the strength of the game's reputation.

Native widescreen + native 360 pad (2010 console-era racer) so no controller/widescreen work. Root english.txt is the language selector (rename to french/italian/german/spanish.txt). Non-Steam -> computed appid. Copy speed note: only 130 files for 7.3GB, so it staged at 106 MB/s — the fastest of the batch, and a neat contrast with HAWX (21,519 files, 6.8GB, 30 MB/s).
